name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-33
title: expand version-alignment invariant (11) to cover script module docstrings
description: Noticed by the Plan C fix-up wave (ws49) while working RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-98. Invariant 11 currently asserts version alignment across three frontmatter files (feature.json, spec.md, contract.md), but rabbit-cage's scripts (e.g. install.py) carry a Version: docstring marker that drifts independently. install.py says 'Version: 5.0.0' even after BUG-98 bumped the spec/feature/contract triple to 5.1.0. Either: (a) extend invariant 11 + test-version-alignment.py to scan every rabbit-cage hook/script for a Version: docstring marker and assert it matches the frontmatter version; (b) remove the Version: docstring markers from scripts entirely, since they duplicate metadata that should live in one place. (b) is cleaner. Pick up in a future rabbit-cage housekeeping audit pass; not blocking.

history.note: Wave 8 audit closes BACKLOG-33 via the spec-level exclusion path. Spec Inv 11 was extended with a justified exclusion clause: script-level Version: docstring markers under hooks/ and scripts/ track their own SemVer and are NOT bound by the three-way feature-version pin. Path (b) from the item description (delete the markers) was over-broad — per-script versions are useful. The exclusion clause makes the existing markers spec-compliant without code change.
